chain.thought: Explanation: The table outlines a three-year rotation of passages: Year 1 (e.g., 2013, 2016, 2019, 2022) reads 6:2–7:7, Year 2 (e.g., 2014, 2017, 2020, 2023) reads 7:8–8:15, and Year 3 (e.g., 2015, 2018, 2021, 2024) reads 8:16–9:35. The cycle repeats every three years, so the same passages are read again after 3 years.

judge_thought: Explanation: The table organizes readings into three distinct yearly cycles (year 1, year 2, year 3), each with unique passage ranges. The listed years for each cycle recur every three years (e.g., year 1: 2013, 2016, 2019, 2022; year 2: 2014, 2017, 2020, 2023; year 3: 2015, 2018, 2021, 2024), indicating a triennial rotation. You avoid repeating passages within the three-year cycle and only encounter the same passages again when the cycle repeats, which takes 3 years.
